signals.reasoning: Existing ETH long remains a small bounded-risk management hold with live reduce-only stop near 2352 and TP near 2458 confirmed on exchange, so this is still not an urgent cleanup exit. The board is under VETO, which argues against rotating into anything new while the current ETH risk is already defined.

BTC is still digesting the prior squeeze and now sits under a harsher geopolitical VETO headline set. That keeps it neutral for position-management purposes rather than a reason to rotate away from the protected ETH hold.
SOL is tied to the same broad risk tape and is not offering a cleaner management setup than the current ETH position. Under VETO it stays watch-only.
TST remains a noisy momentum name after a sharp squeeze-style move, which makes fresh action lower quality under an active event brake. No reason to prefer it over simply managing ETH.
STBL can still be mechanically interesting, but it is too unstable and too headline-sensitive to outrank the existing bounded-risk ETH hold. Keep it on the watchlist only.
PEOPLE looks more like recovery noise than a clean fresh trend. Position-management priority plus VETO keeps it in the hold bucket.
FOGO is exactly the kind of thin high-beta name that becomes expensive to manage when headlines are driving the board. Not a cleaner place to be than the current protected ETH position.
VVV remains too unstable and event-sensitive to trust as a fresh management rotation. It stays watch-only while ETH remains the active managed exposure.
BABY is another thin, reactive name where execution quality can deteriorate fast under VETO. That keeps it out of the action set.
LDO still has some constructive rebound structure, but not enough separation to justify rotating away from ETH. Under VETO it remains a watchlist hold.
MERL is still in rebound mode without becoming a clearly superior opportunity. Better to manage the open ETH position than to force a switch here.
